news.headline: HK's regulator releases "ASPIRe" roadmap with 12 initiatives to improve virtual asset ecosystem.
news.summary: The SFC has introduced the "ASPIRe" roadmap, which details 12 essential strategies aimed at improving market access, security, and product offerings.

news.headline: Elon Musk's department accessing federal agencies' data sparks legal battles over Americans' personal info.
news.summary: Staff members from Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency are currently obtaining extensive data from federal agencies, despite facing legal challenges seeking to prevent their access.

news.headline: New capital-raising method, perpetual strike preferred stocks, aims to fund Bitcoin purchases with priority dividends, conversion options, and controlled dilution.
news.summary: The most recent method used by Strategy to raise capital involves perpetual strike preferred stock. This method is specifically created to finance Bitcoin purchases and offers priority dividends, conversion options, and controlled dilution.

news.headline: Russia’s Lavrov: We have begun to move away from the edge of the abyss where Biden's administration led our relationship.
Russia’s Foreign Minister Lavrov: Trump's remarks on NATO being the main reason for the conflict in Ukraine are a signal that he understands our position.
METLEN Energy & Metals partners with Rio Tinto for long-term agreements in Bauxite and Alumina, improving supply chain.
news.summary: In Athens, Greece on February 19, 2025, METLEN Energy & Metals (METLEN) has recently finalized two significant long-term strategic partnerships with Rio Tinto. These agreements focus on optimizing the supply chains for Bauxite and Alumina. This move is expected to bring substantial operational enhancements to both companies. The specifics of the agreements were shared in a previous announcement.

ST Engineering iDirect CEO Don Claussen nominated for Via Satellite's Satellite Executive of the Year Award in 2024 for his innovation in advancing the industry.
news.summary: ST Engineering iDirect, a prominent player in the satellite communications industry, recently highlighted the key role of their CEO, Don Claussen, in fostering innovation to propel the sector forward. The recognition was bestowed by Via Satellite in Herndon, Virginia, on February 19, 2025. Don Claussen's leadership has been instrumental in driving advancements in the industry, with ST Engineering iDirect being recognized as a global leader in satellite communications. This recognition underscores the company's commitment to driving innovation under Don Claussen's leadership.

news.headline: TNL Mediagene (NASDAQ: TNMG) leads Asian market with 3 group companies nominated for 4 major awards.
news.summary: In an announcement made on February 19, 2025, in New York and Taipei, TNL Mediagene, listed under the ticker symbol TNMG on NASDAQ, revealed that three of its subsidiaries have been nominated for all the major awards at the "Agency & Advertiser of the Year Awards 2024." This recognition highlights the success of Asia's progressive digital media conglomerate, with a strong presence in the industry.

news.headline: Nippon Steel Solutions invests in Momenta's Digital Industry IV fund, advancing Industry 5.0. Tokyo, Feb. 19, 2025.
news.summary: In Lucerne, Switzerland and Tokyo, a significant announcement was made on February 19, 2025. Momenta Ventures shared that Nippon Steel Solutions, a prominent provider of cutting-edge IT solutions in Japan, has decided to make a strategic investment in Momenta's Digital Industry IV venture capital fund. This move reflects a substantial step forward in strengthening technological advancements and innovation in the industry. Momenta is excited about the partnership, highlighting the growing importance of digital transformations.

news.headline: Lumeris unveils Tom™ AI system at ViVE 2025, tackling 2-billion-hour primary care gap by improving patient access.
news.summary: At the ViVE 2025 event in Nashville, a groundbreaking Primary Care as a Service™ solution was introduced by Lumeris on Feb. 19, 2025. Addressing the significant 2-billion-hour primary care gap, this innovative offering features an AI-powered care team member. This latest advancement aims to enhance capacity, alleviate burdens, and enhance patient care access in the healthcare industry.

news.headline: New ezAccounting software by Halfpricesoft.com is out, with password protection for multi-user access.
news.summary: A new version of ezAccounting business software has been launched by developers at Halfpricesoft.com in Redmond, Washington on February 19, 2025. The updated software now includes password protection features to allow multiple users restricted access to specific areas. This enhancement comes after hearing customer feedback, showcasing the commitment to user satisfaction.

news.headline: Small town divided by cryptocurrency scam, causing tension among residents.
news.summary: In the latest update on the COVID-19 pandemic, global cases have reached over 200 million, with the death toll surpassing 4.2 million. The Delta variant continues to drive surges in various countries, leading to increased concerns. In the United States alone, over 35 million cases have been reported, with more than 614,000 deaths. Vaccination efforts are ongoing, with over 4.5 billion doses administered worldwide. Despite this progress, disparities in vaccine distribution and hesitancy remain significant challenges in achieving herd immunity. Public health authorities emphasize the importance of continued preventive measures to curb the spread of the virus.

news.headline: Medigene AG receives U.S. patent for JOVI technology in cancer treatment, involving anti-Cβ antibody.
news.summary: On February 19, 2025, Medigene AG, a company specializing in oncology research (FSE: MDG1, Prime Standard), revealed that it has obtained a patent from the U.S. Patent Office safeguarding its JOVI technology. This technology facilitates the enrichment of T cells through the application of a specialized anti-Cβ antibody. The patent for the JOVI technology was officially granted in late last year, with Medigene receiving confirmation of this development in 2025.

news.headline: Regulators faulted for LIBRA memecoin uproar – Coin Bureau founder. Outcry over unclear legality of memecoins like Libra, which crumbled post-endorsement by Argentine President Javier Milei.
news.summary: The lack of regulatory clarity surrounding memecoins, such as Libra, has sparked outrage within the cryptocurrency community. After being endorsed by Argentine President Javier Milei, Libra quickly collapsed, leaving many questioning its legitimacy. This incident has raised concerns about the legal framework governing cryptocurrencies and their susceptibility to manipulation. Members of the crypto community are expressing frustration over these issues, emphasizing the need for clearer guidelines in this evolving market.

news.headline: Milesight to launch G2 Series at 2025 event in Xiamen, China, Feb. 19, promising new standards in video surveillance.
news.summary: Milesight, a top player in IP-based video surveillance solutions, has recently introduced its G2 Series during the 2025 New Product Launch Event in Xiamen, China. This launch marks a significant milestone in setting new benchmarks for video surveillance, intelligent traffic systems, and outdoor monitoring. The G2 Series showcases cutting-edge technology aimed at revolutionizing the industry landscape, promising enhanced performance and advanced features. The event generated considerable interest, with attendees and stakeholders eager to experience the latest advancements.

news.headline: MainStreaming featured in 2024 Gartner® report for Industry Edge benefits in Communications and Media. Award-winning video delivery network.
news.summary: On February 19, 2025, MainStreaming, a leading Edge Video Delivery Network, was acknowledged in the 2024 Gartner® Innovation Insight for Industry Edge's role in boosting productivity in the Communications and Media sector. This recognition highlights MainStreaming's commitment to innovation and industry leadership in utilizing edge technology.

news.headline: Mercedes-Benz of Arrowhead in Peoria, Arizona, offers special February leases on select 2024 and 2025 models, benefiting luxury vehicle enthusiasts.
news.summary: In Peoria, Arizona, Mercedes-Benz of Arrowhead has unveiled special leasing deals for the month of February. This promotion presents an opportunity for luxury car lovers to enjoy driving various 2024 and 2025 models of Mercedes-Benz at highly competitive rates. This offer provides a unique chance for customers in Peoria to experience top-notch luxury vehicles at affordable prices.

news.headline: Repsol partners with IBS Software to overhaul logistics ops; adopting iLogistics Control Tower.
news.summary: In Madrid on February 19, 2025, Repsol, a prominent global integrated energy firm, announced a collaboration with IBS Software to enhance its primary logistics operations in various sites. This strategic partnership will involve Repsol implementing IBS Software's iLogistics Control Tower system, aiming to revolutionize its logistics processes.

news.headline: Acadian Plant Health™ introduces Abiotic Stress Management portfolio on Feb. 19, 2025, surpassing biostimulants with enhanced plant growth and stress tolerance.
news.summary: In Dartmouth, Nova Scotia, on February 19th, 2025, Acadian Plant Health introduced its innovative Abiotic Stress Management (ASM) portfolio, marking a significant leap ahead in the industry compared to existing biostimulants. The ASM portfolio has shown consistent effectiveness in improving plant growth and boosting stress resilience, setting a new standard in the sector.

news.headline: Elliott Investment Management holds $1.5 billion investment in Aspen Technology, issues statement.
news.summary: In West Palm Beach, Florida on February 19, 2025, Elliott Investment Management L.P. ("Elliott") disclosed that the funds under its management hold investments exceeding $1.5 billion in Aspen Technology, Inc. (NASDAQ: AZPN). The statement was released by Elliott regarding AspenTech's current situation.

news.headline: HydrogenPro ASA - Invitation to Q4 2024 webcast
news.summary: The financial results for the fourth quarter of 2024 by HydrogenPro ASA (OSE: HYPRO) are set to be released on Tuesday, February 25, 2025, at 07:00 CET. Following this, the company will conduct a webcast presentation of the results at 10:00 CET on the same day, including a Q&A session.

news.summary: A recent report reveals that over 2,000 individuals in the city have been affected by a new flu outbreak, with 15 reported as being in critical condition. Health officials are urging the public to take preventative measures such as getting vaccinated and practicing good hygiene to curb the spread of the virus. The increase in flu cases has led to a surge in hospital admissions, with local hospitals reaching 90% capacity. The city has allocated $500,000 in emergency funds to combat the outbreak and provide necessary medical resources. Residents are advised to stay informed and follow health guidelines to protect themselves and others during this flu season.

news.headline: Auction results: 2.25% DGB 2035 and 0.25% DGB 2052 sold.
news.summary: Last night, a fire broke out in a residential building in downtown, resulting in the evacuation of over 100 residents. The blaze, which started on the fourth floor, was brought under control by firefighters after three hours of tackling the flames. Sadly, two people were injured and were taken to the hospital for treatment. The extent of the damage is still being assessed, but preliminary estimates suggest that over $500,000 worth of property has been lost. The cause of the fire is yet to be determined, and investigations are ongoing. Residents have been offered temporary accommodation by local authorities while the building undergoes repairs.

news.headline: Michael Saylor plans to raise $2 billion in convertible debt to buy more Bitcoin. The notes will mature in 2030 and can be converted into cash, stock, or both at Strategy's discretion. The company can redeem the notes for cash starting in 2027 if the stock price stays above 130% of the conversion price.
news.summary: The largest corporate Bitcoin holder globally, Michael Saylor's Strategy (NASDAQ:MSTR), plans to issue $2 billion in convertible debt through a private offering with an additional $300 million option for early buyers. These notes, maturing on March 1, 2030, can convert into cash, stock, or a combination of both at the company's discretion. There will be no regular interest on the notes, with their principal amount remaining constant. Strategy can redeem the notes for cash from March 5, 2027, onwards if its stock price stays 130% above the conversion price for a specific period. Investors have the right to request more information.

Arthur D. Little study: AI will transform development and engineering, organizations must reshape themselves for success.
news.summary: In London, it has been reported that the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) will bring about significant changes in development and engineering operations. To achieve success in this endeavor, companies must undergo a restructuring process. This shift towards AI is expected to have a substantial impact on the way organizations operate.

news.headline: Broadband Forum in Calif. renewing focus on services-led broadband with 5 new work areas to meet user demands.
news.summary: In Fremont, California, Broadband Forum has restructured its focus to prioritize projects and specifications that support user-demanded services and intelligent broadband. The organization's revamped work areas, now totalling five, will address access networks, connected users, subscriber networks, network architecture, cloud and autonomous networks, and service requirements, all aimed at driving the development of key applications and services. This shift will ensure a sharper emphasis on meeting user needs and promoting innovative broadband solutions.

news.headline: Wintermute set to open a New York office, aiming for growth in OTC and derivatives. CEO sees potential in Trump's crypto-friendly approach.
news.summary: Wintermute, a company specializing in cryptocurrency, is set to expand its operations in the US with the opening of a new office in New York. CEO Evgeny Gaevoy expressed confidence in this move, attributing it to the favorable crypto policies expected under the Trump administration. This decision reflects Wintermute's strategic vision to capitalize on the growing interest in digital currencies.

Solar A/S Annual General Meeting on March 14, 2025, at 11 am, Industrivej Vest 43, Vejen, Denmark.
news.summary: Solar A/S is scheduled to conduct its Annual General Meeting on Friday, March 14, 2025, starting at 11:00 am. The meeting venue is located at Industrivej Vest 43, Vejen, Denmark (postal code: 6600).

news.headline: MFH's subsidiary Aifinity Base Ltd to produce liquid cooling solutions for Nvidia® chip-powered AI data centers.
news.summary: In a recent announcement on Feb. 19, 2025, Mercurity Fintech Holding Inc. (MFH) disclosed the establishment of a majority-owned subsidiary named Aifinity Base Limited in Hong Kong. Aifinity aims to produce specialized liquid cooling panels designed for artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure, high-performance computing (HPC), with a focus on enhancing the efficiency and performance of Nvidia® chip-powered GPUs and other high-performance AI accelerators. The Company, listed on Nasdaq under the ticker MFH, is venturing into this expansion to cater to the growing demand for technological advancements in AI and computing sectors.

CZ gifts 150 BNB to Libra scam victims, ends up with more than donated; will contribute extra crypto received.
news.summary: Changpeng "CZ" Zhao, the founder of Binance, has announced plans to donate surplus cryptocurrency he received to aid the victims of the LIBRA scam.

Carvana to report Q4 earnings of 29 cents/share, revenue $3.31B; shares closed at $284.53, down 0.3%. Wall Street forecasts positive outlook for the company.
news.summary: Carvana Co. (NYSE:CVNA) is set to announce its Q4 financial results on Feb. 19, 2025, after market close. Analysts are predicting earnings of 29 cents per share, contrasting with a $1.00 loss per share in the same period last year. The company is expected to report quarterly revenue of $3.31 billion, up from $2.42 billion a year ago. Carvana has surpassed revenue projections in the last three consecutive quarters and in five of the past ten quarters. On Tuesday, Carvana's stock saw a negligible decrease of 0.3%, closing at $284.53. For more details and the full story, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: DMEGC Solar in China wins EcoVadis Silver Medal for sustainability evaluation on Feb. 17, 2025 as part of Hengdian Group.
news.summary: In Jinhua, China, on February 17, 2025, DMEGC Solar, a PV module manufacturer under Hengdian Group, received the EcoVadis Silver Medal after undergoing a thorough assessment for corporate social responsibility. This internationally recognized award highlights the company's commitment to sustainability practices.

news.headline: Tickets for the FORMULA 1® ETIHAD AIRWAYS ABU DHABI GRAND PRIX 2025 are now on sale at Yas Marina Circuit.
news.summary: In Abu Dhabi, UAE, on February 19, 2025, Ethara has announced the commencement of ticket sales for the 2025 Formula 1® season's concluding event, the FORMULA 1® ETIHAD AIRWAYS ABU DHABI GRAND PRIX 2025. Fans are invited to secure their tickets to witness the exciting season climax at the renowned Yas Marina Circuit.

news.headline: CKGSB and Johns Hopkins University collaborate on AI-Driven Healthcare Innovation Program, launching on Feb. 19, 2025.
news.summary: On February 19, 2025, in Beijing, the Cheung Kong Graduate School of Business (CKGSB) and Johns Hopkins University's Carey Business School revealed the introduction of the groundbreaking AI-Driven Healthcare Innovation Program. This collaborative effort aims to support senior professionals and entrepreneurs in the healthcare sector.
